member.name: T:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Internal.AmazonSSOOIDCEndpointResolver
member.summary: Amazon SSOOIDC endpoint resolver. Custom PipelineHandler responsible for resolving endpoint and setting authentication parameters for SSOOIDC service requests. Collects values for SSOOIDCEndpointParameters and then tries to resolve endpoint by calling ResolveEndpoint method on GlobalEndpoints.Provider if present, otherwise uses SSOOIDCEndpointProvider. Responsible for setting authentication and http headers provided by resolved endpoint.

member.name: T:Amazon.SSOOIDC.AmazonSSOOIDCClient
member.summary:
Implementation for accessing SSOOIDC
IAM Identity Center OpenID Connect (OIDC) is a web service that enables a client (such as CLI or a native application) to register with IAM Identity Center. The service also enables the client to fetch the user’s access token upon successful authentication and authorization with IAM Identity Center.
IAM Identity Center uses the sso and identitystore API namespaces.
Considerations for Using This Guide
Before you begin using this guide, we recommend that you first review the following important information about how the IAM Identity Center OIDC service works.
The IAM Identity Center OIDC service currently implements only the portions of the OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant standard (https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc8628) that are necessary to enable single sign-on authentication with the CLI.
With older versions of the CLI, the service only emits OIDC access tokens, so to obtain a new token, users must explicitly re-authenticate. To access the OIDC flow that supports token refresh and doesn’t require re-authentication, update to the latest CLI version (1.27.10 for CLI V1 and 2.9.0 for CLI V2) with support for OIDC token refresh and configurable IAM Identity Center session durations. For more information, see Configure Amazon Web Services access portal session duration .
The access tokens provided by this service grant access to all Amazon Web Services account entitlements assigned to an IAM Identity Center user, not just a particular application.
The documentation in this guide does not describe the mechanism to convert the access token into Amazon Web Services Auth (“sigv4”) credentials for use with IAM-protected Amazon Web Services service endpoints. For more information, see GetRoleCredentials in the IAM Identity Center Portal API Reference Guide.
For general information about IAM Identity Center, see What is IAM Identity Center? in the IAM Identity Center User Guide.

member.name: M:Amazon.SSOOIDC.AmazonSSOOIDCClient.#ctor
member.summary:
Constructs AmazonSSOOIDCClient with the credentials loaded from the application's default configuration, and if unsuccessful from the Instance Profile service on an EC2 instance.
Example App.config with credentials set.
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?> <configuration> <appSettings> <add key="AWSProfileName" value="AWS Default"/> </appSettings> </configuration>

member.name: M:Amazon.SSOOIDC.AmazonSSOOIDCClient.CreateTokenAsync(Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenRequest,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Creates and returns access and refresh tokens for clients that are authenticated using client secrets. The access token can be used to fetch short-term credentials for the assigned AWS accounts or to access application APIs using bearer authentication.
member.param: Container for the necessary parameters to execute the CreateToken service method.
A cancellation token that can be used by other objects or threads to receive notice of cancellation.
member.returns: The response from the CreateToken service method, as returned by SSOOIDC.
member.exception: You do not have sufficient access to perform this action.
Indicates that a request to authorize a client with an access user session token is pending.
Indicates that the token issued by the service is expired and is no longer valid.
Indicates that an error from the service occurred while trying to process a request.
Indicates that the clientId or clientSecret in the request is invalid. For example, this can occur when a client sends an incorrect clientId or an expired clientSecret.
Indicates that a request contains an invalid grant. This can occur if a client makes a CreateToken request with an invalid grant type.
Indicates that something is wrong with the input to the request. For example, a required parameter might be missing or out of range.
Indicates that the scope provided in the request is invalid.
Indicates that the client is making the request too frequently and is more than the service can handle.
Indicates that the client is not currently authorized to make the request. This can happen when a clientId is not issued for a public client.
Indicates that the grant type in the request is not supported by the service.
member.seealso: REST API Reference for CreateToken Operation

member.name: T:Amazon.SSOOIDC.IAmazonSSOOIDC
member.summary:
Interface for accessing SSOOIDC
IAM Identity Center OpenID Connect (OIDC) is a web service that enables a client (such as CLI or a native application) to register with IAM Identity Center. The service also enables the client to fetch the user’s access token upon successful authentication and authorization with IAM Identity Center.
IAM Identity Center uses the sso and identitystore API namespaces.
Considerations for Using This Guide
Before you begin using this guide, we recommend that you first review the following important information about how the IAM Identity Center OIDC service works.
The IAM Identity Center OIDC service currently implements only the portions of the OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant standard (https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc8628) that are necessary to enable single sign-on authentication with the CLI.
With older versions of the CLI, the service only emits OIDC access tokens, so to obtain a new token, users must explicitly re-authenticate. To access the OIDC flow that supports token refresh and doesn’t require re-authentication, update to the latest CLI version (1.27.10 for CLI V1 and 2.9.0 for CLI V2) with support for OIDC token refresh and configurable IAM Identity Center session durations. For more information, see Configure Amazon Web Services access portal session duration .
The access tokens provided by this service grant access to all Amazon Web Services account entitlements assigned to an IAM Identity Center user, not just a particular application.
The documentation in this guide does not describe the mechanism to convert the access token into Amazon Web Services Auth (“sigv4”) credentials for use with IAM-protected Amazon Web Services service endpoints. For more information, see GetRoleCredentials in the IAM Identity Center Portal API Reference Guide.
For general information about IAM Identity Center, see What is IAM Identity Center? in the IAM Identity Center User Guide.

member.name: M:Amazon.SSOOIDC.AmazonSSOOIDCDefaultConfiguration.GetAllConfigurations
member.summary: Collection of all s supported by SSOOIDC

summary.p: The STANDARD mode provides the latest recommended default values that should be safe to run in most scenarios
Note that the default values vended from this mode might change as best practices may evolve. As a result, it is encouraged to perform tests when upgrading the SDK
The IN_REGION mode builds on the standard mode and includes optimization tailored for applications which call AWS services from within the same AWS region
Note that the default values vended from this mode might change as best practices may evolve. As a result, it is encouraged to perform tests when upgrading the SDK
The CROSS_REGION mode builds on the standard mode and includes optimization tailored for applications which call AWS services in a different region
Note that the default values vended from this mode might change as best practices may evolve. As a result, it is encouraged to perform tests when upgrading the SDK
The MOBILE mode builds on the standard mode and includes optimization tailored for mobile applications
Note that the default values vended from this mode might change as best practices may evolve. As a result, it is encouraged to perform tests when upgrading the SDK
The AUTO mode is an experimental mode that builds on the standard mode. The SDK will attempt to discover the execution environment to determine the appropriate settings automatically.

Note that the auto detection is heuristics-based and does not guarantee 100% accuracy. STANDARD mode will be used if the execution environment cannot be determined. The auto detection might query EC2 Instance Metadata service, which might introduce latency. Therefore we recommend choosing an explicit defaults_mode instead if startup latency is critical to your application
The LEGACY mode provides default settings that vary per SDK and were used prior to establishment of defaults_mode

member.name: T:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenRequest
member.summary: Container for the parameters to the CreateToken operation. Creates and returns access and refresh tokens for clients that are authenticated using client secrets. The access token can be used to fetch short-term credentials for the assigned AWS accounts or to access application APIs using bearer authentication.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenRequest.ClientId
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property ClientId.
The unique identifier string for the client or application. This value comes from the result of the RegisterClient API.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenRequest.ClientSecret
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property ClientSecret.
A secret string generated for the client. This value should come from the persisted result of the RegisterClient API.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenRequest.Code
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property Code.
Used only when calling this API for the Authorization Code grant type. The short-term code is used to identify this authorization request. This grant type is currently unsupported for the CreateToken API.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenRequest.CodeVerifier
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property CodeVerifier.
Used only when calling this API for the Authorization Code grant type. This value is generated by the client and presented to validate the original code challenge value the client passed at authorization time.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenRequest.DeviceCode
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property DeviceCode.
Used only when calling this API for the Device Code grant type. This short-term code is used to identify this authorization request. This comes from the result of the StartDeviceAuthorization API.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenRequest.GrantType
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property GrantType.
Supports the following OAuth grant types: Device Code and Refresh Token. Specify either of the following values, depending on the grant type that you want:
* Device Code - urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:device_code
* Refresh Token - refresh_token
For information about how to obtain the device code, see the StartDeviceAuthorization topic.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenRequest.RedirectUri
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property RedirectUri.
Used only when calling this API for the Authorization Code grant type. This value specifies the location of the client or application that has registered to receive the authorization code.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenRequest.RefreshToken
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property RefreshToken.
Used only when calling this API for the Refresh Token grant type. This token is used to refresh short-term tokens, such as the access token, that might expire.
For more information about the features and limitations of the current IAM Identity Center OIDC implementation, see Considerations for Using this Guide in the IAM Identity Center OIDC API Reference.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenRequest.Scope
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property Scope.
The list of scopes for which authorization is requested. The access token that is issued is limited to the scopes that are granted. If this value is not specified, IAM Identity Center authorizes all scopes that are configured for the client during the call to RegisterClient.

member.name: M:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenRequest.CreateSigner
member.summary: Get the signer to use for this request.

member.name: T:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenResponse
member.summary: This is the response object from the CreateToken operation.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenResponse.AccessToken
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property AccessToken.
A bearer token to access Amazon Web Services accounts and applications assigned to a user.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenResponse.ExpiresIn
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property ExpiresIn.
Indicates the time in seconds when an access token will expire.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenResponse.IdToken
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property IdToken.
The idToken is not implemented or supported. For more information about the features and limitations of the current IAM Identity Center OIDC implementation, see Considerations for Using this Guide in the IAM Identity Center OIDC API Reference.
A JSON Web Token (JWT) that identifies who is associated with the issued access token.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenResponse.RefreshToken
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property RefreshToken.
A token that, if present, can be used to refresh a previously issued access token that might have expired.
For more information about the features and limitations of the current IAM Identity Center OIDC implementation, see Considerations for Using this Guide in the IAM Identity Center OIDC API Reference.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenResponse.TokenType
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property TokenType.
Used to notify the client that the returned token is an access token. The supported token type is Bearer.

member.name: T:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenWithIAMRequest
member.summary: Container for the parameters to the CreateTokenWithIAM operation. Creates and returns access and refresh tokens for clients and applications that are authenticated using IAM entities. The access token can be used to fetch short-term credentials for the assigned Amazon Web Services accounts or to access application APIs using bearer authentication.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenWithIAMRequest.Assertion
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property Assertion.
Used only when calling this API for the JWT Bearer grant type. This value specifies the JSON Web Token (JWT) issued by a trusted token issuer. To authorize a trusted token issuer, configure the JWT Bearer GrantOptions for the application.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenWithIAMRequest.ClientId
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property ClientId.
The unique identifier string for the client or application. This value is an application ARN that has OAuth grants configured.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenWithIAMRequest.Code
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property Code.
Used only when calling this API for the Authorization Code grant type. This short-term code is used to identify this authorization request. The code is obtained through a redirect from IAM Identity Center to a redirect URI persisted in the Authorization Code GrantOptions for the application.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenWithIAMRequest.CodeVerifier
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property CodeVerifier.
Used only when calling this API for the Authorization Code grant type. This value is generated by the client and presented to validate the original code challenge value the client passed at authorization time.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenWithIAMRequest.GrantType
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property GrantType.
Supports the following OAuth grant types: Authorization Code, Refresh Token, JWT Bearer, and Token Exchange. Specify one of the following values, depending on the grant type that you want:
* Authorization Code - authorization_code
* Refresh Token - refresh_token
* JWT Bearer - urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:jwt-bearer
* Token Exchange - urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:token-exchange

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenWithIAMRequest.RedirectUri
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property RedirectUri.
Used only when calling this API for the Authorization Code grant type. This value specifies the location of the client or application that has registered to receive the authorization code.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenWithIAMRequest.RefreshToken
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property RefreshToken.
Used only when calling this API for the Refresh Token grant type. This token is used to refresh short-term tokens, such as the access token, that might expire.
For more information about the features and limitations of the current IAM Identity Center OIDC implementation, see Considerations for Using this Guide in the IAM Identity Center OIDC API Reference.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenWithIAMRequest.RequestedTokenType
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property RequestedTokenType.
Used only when calling this API for the Token Exchange grant type. This value specifies the type of token that the requester can receive. The following values are supported:
* Access Token - urn:ietf:params:oauth:token-type:access_token
* Refresh Token - urn:ietf:params:oauth:token-type:refresh_token

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenWithIAMRequest.Scope
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property Scope.
The list of scopes for which authorization is requested. The access token that is issued is limited to the scopes that are granted. If the value is not specified, IAM Identity Center authorizes all scopes configured for the application, including the following default scopes: openid, aws, sts:identity_context.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenWithIAMRequest.SubjectToken
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property SubjectToken.
Used only when calling this API for the Token Exchange grant type. This value specifies the subject of the exchange. The value of the subject token must be an access token issued by IAM Identity Center to a different client or application. The access token must have authorized scopes that indicate the requested application as a target audience.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenWithIAMRequest.SubjectTokenType
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property SubjectTokenType.
Used only when calling this API for the Token Exchange grant type. This value specifies the type of token that is passed as the subject of the exchange. The following value is supported:
* Access Token - urn:ietf:params:oauth:token-type:access_token

member.name: T:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenWithIAMResponse
member.summary: This is the response object from the CreateTokenWithIAM operation.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenWithIAMResponse.AccessToken
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property AccessToken.
A bearer token to access Amazon Web Services accounts and applications assigned to a user.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenWithIAMResponse.ExpiresIn
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property ExpiresIn.
Indicates the time in seconds when an access token will expire.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenWithIAMResponse.IdToken
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property IdToken.
A JSON Web Token (JWT) that identifies the user associated with the issued access token.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenWithIAMResponse.IssuedTokenType
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property IssuedTokenType.
Indicates the type of tokens that are issued by IAM Identity Center. The following values are supported:
* Access Token - urn:ietf:params:oauth:token-type:access_token
* Refresh Token - urn:ietf:params:oauth:token-type:refresh_token

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenWithIAMResponse.RefreshToken
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property RefreshToken.
A token that, if present, can be used to refresh a previously issued access token that might have expired.
For more information about the features and limitations of the current IAM Identity Center OIDC implementation, see Considerations for Using this Guide in the IAM Identity Center OIDC API Reference.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenWithIAMResponse.Scope
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property Scope.
The list of scopes for which authorization is granted. The access token that is issued is limited to the scopes that are granted.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.CreateTokenWithIAMResponse.TokenType
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property TokenType.
Used to notify the requester that the returned token is an access token. The supported token type is Bearer.

member.name: T:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.RegisterClientRequest
member.summary: Container for the parameters to the RegisterClient operation. Registers a client with IAM Identity Center. This allows clients to initiate device authorization. The output should be persisted for reuse through many authentication requests.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.RegisterClientRequest.ClientName
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property ClientName.
The friendly name of the client.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.RegisterClientRequest.ClientType
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property ClientType.
The type of client. The service supports only public as a client type. Anything other than public will be rejected by the service.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.RegisterClientRequest.EntitledApplicationArn
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property EntitledApplicationArn.
This IAM Identity Center application ARN is used to define administrator-managed configuration for public client access to resources. At authorization, the scopes, grants, and redirect URI available to this client will be restricted by this application resource.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.RegisterClientRequest.GrantTypes
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property GrantTypes.
The list of OAuth 2.0 grant types that are defined by the client. This list is used to restrict the token granting flows available to the client.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.RegisterClientRequest.IssuerUrl
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property IssuerUrl.
The IAM Identity Center Issuer URL associated with an instance of IAM Identity Center. This value is needed for user access to resources through the client.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.RegisterClientRequest.RedirectUris
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property RedirectUris.
The list of redirect URI that are defined by the client. At completion of authorization, this list is used to restrict what locations the user agent can be redirected back to.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.RegisterClientRequest.Scopes
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property Scopes.
The list of scopes that are defined by the client. Upon authorization, this list is used to restrict permissions when granting an access token.

member.name: M:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.RegisterClientRequest.CreateSigner
member.summary: Get the signer to use for this request.

member.name: T:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.RegisterClientResponse
member.summary: This is the response object from the RegisterClient operation.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.RegisterClientResponse.AuthorizationEndpoint
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property AuthorizationEndpoint.
An endpoint that the client can use to request authorization.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.RegisterClientResponse.ClientId
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property ClientId.
The unique identifier string for each client. This client uses this identifier to get authenticated by the service in subsequent calls.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.RegisterClientResponse.ClientIdIssuedAt
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property ClientIdIssuedAt.
Indicates the time at which the clientId and clientSecret were issued.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.RegisterClientResponse.ClientSecret
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property ClientSecret.
A secret string generated for the client. The client will use this string to get authenticated by the service in subsequent calls.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.RegisterClientResponse.ClientSecretExpiresAt
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property ClientSecretExpiresAt.
Indicates the time at which the clientId and clientSecret will become invalid.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.RegisterClientResponse.TokenEndpoint
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property TokenEndpoint.
An endpoint that the client can use to create tokens.

member.name: T:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.StartDeviceAuthorizationRequest
member.summary: Container for the parameters to the StartDeviceAuthorization operation. Initiates device authorization by requesting a pair of verification codes from the authorization service.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.StartDeviceAuthorizationRequest.ClientId
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property ClientId.
The unique identifier string for the client that is registered with IAM Identity Center. This value should come from the persisted result of the RegisterClient API operation.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.StartDeviceAuthorizationRequest.ClientSecret
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property ClientSecret.
A secret string that is generated for the client. This value should come from the persisted result of the RegisterClient API operation.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.StartDeviceAuthorizationRequest.StartUrl
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property StartUrl.
The URL for the Amazon Web Services access portal. For more information, see Using the Amazon Web Services access portal in the IAM Identity Center User Guide.

member.name: M:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.StartDeviceAuthorizationRequest.CreateSigner
member.summary: Get the signer to use for this request.

member.name: T:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.StartDeviceAuthorizationResponse
member.summary: This is the response object from the StartDeviceAuthorization operation.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.StartDeviceAuthorizationResponse.DeviceCode
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property DeviceCode.
The short-lived code that is used by the device when polling for a session token.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.StartDeviceAuthorizationResponse.ExpiresIn
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property ExpiresIn.
Indicates the number of seconds in which the verification code will become invalid.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.StartDeviceAuthorizationResponse.Interval
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property Interval.
Indicates the number of seconds the client must wait between attempts when polling for a session.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.StartDeviceAuthorizationResponse.UserCode
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property UserCode.
A one-time user verification code. This is needed to authorize an in-use device.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.StartDeviceAuthorizationResponse.VerificationUri
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property VerificationUri.
The URI of the verification page that takes the userCode to authorize the device.

member.name: P:Amazon.SSOOIDC.Model.StartDeviceAuthorizationResponse.VerificationUriComplete
member.summary:
Gets and sets the property VerificationUriComplete.
An alternate URL that the client can use to automatically launch a browser. This process skips the manual step in which the user visits the verification page and enters their code.
